tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting impaired renal function, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease. Elevated urine glucose, ketones, and bilirubin may indicate metabolic disturbances or possible liver involvement. Low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further nephrological and metabolic evaluation, including assessment for urinary tract infection, diabetes, and liver function.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
